title: The Pesticide Still Legal on American Crops Doubles Your Risk of Parkinson's. We Now Know Why.
description: A landmark UCLA study — the largest epidemiological cohort of its kind, corroborated by mouse and zebrafish models — has identified chlorpyrifos, still legally sprayed on US crops and registered in over 100 countries, as a likely causal trigger for Parkinson's disease, with a 2.68-fold increased risk in exposed populations. The mechanism is specific and damning: chlorpyrifos disables the brain's protein waste-disposal system, allowing toxic alpha-synuclein to accumulate into the Lewy bodies that kill dopaminergic neurons. With 85% of Parkinson's cases having no known cause and the disease growing faster than any other neurological disorder, this may be the modifiable risk factor the field has been searching for — and regulators have been slow to act on.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Pesticide Still Legal on American Crops Doubles Your Risk of Parkinson's. We Now Know Why.
